endings.title: Blacksite Burn
endings.summary: The smuggler cache seals shut around you and the station hands your signal to the navy.

endings.title: Counter-Hacked
endings.summary: A rival operator lures you into a duel, strips your toolkit, and sells your route map.

endings.title: Dockside Snare
endings.summary: Alecto's docking spine floods with kill-light and the boarding team closes around you.

endings.title: Cold Vacuum
endings.summary: You win the corridor, then lose the ship. Alecto opens you to space like a warning.

endings.title: Pact with the Watcher
endings.summary: You leave alive, but a fragment of the patrol AI leaves with you and starts making demands.

endings.title: Mutiny by Whisper
endings.summary: Prisoners and deckhands seize Alecto while you ghost out with their confessions and the admiral's ledgers.

endings.title: Alecto Falls Burning
endings.summary: The reactor sings itself apart. Your theft survives. So does the body count.

endings.title: Ledger Tyrant
endings.summary: You do not expose the corruption. You own it, and every captain in range knows your handle.

endings.title: Poisoned by a Peer
endings.summary: The rival hacker leaves a smiling worm in your stack. The last thing you steal is your own death.

endings.title: Inside the Hull
endings.summary: You keep crawling until the ship stops feeling built and starts feeling hungry.

endings.title: The Leak that Started a War
endings.summary: You broadcast the files freely. Whole colonies rise, and fleets begin to burn over the truth.

endings.title: Ghosted the Cruiser
endings.summary: You take the ledger, erase your footsteps, and leave Alecto hunting a phantom.

endings.title: Walked Out in Uniform
endings.summary: Sometimes the cleanest hack is social. You leave through the front gate wearing their badge.

endings.title: Archive of Knives
endings.summary: Vanta's prison records crack open under your touch. Every secret cell in the sector now has a witness.

endings.title: Wrong Side of the Jump
endings.summary: You ride the prison ship's jump core during an illegal burn and arrive nowhere that physics recognizes.

endings.title: Warden's Example
endings.summary: The warden does not question you twice. Your body becomes a lesson for the next intruder.

endings.title: The Clone Keeps Working
endings.summary: You escape, but only because a flash-grown copy of you stays behind and keeps answering to your name.

endings.title: Silent Extraction
endings.summary: You peel out with the prison manifest, the black budget routes, and not a single confirmed sighting.

endings.title: Cells Open
endings.summary: Every locked voice on Vanta finds air at once. The ship becomes a revolution with engines.

endings.title: Ghost Carrier
endings.summary: You surf a dead-channel convoy hidden inside Vanta's sensors and vanish into rumor.

endings.title: Manufactured Enemy
endings.summary: You rewrite the prison records so convincingly that two governments start hunting each other instead of you.

endings.title: Perfect Preservation
endings.summary: The stasis coffin closes before you understand it is the room, not the device, that is the trap.

endings.title: Knife with a Handshake
endings.summary: You split the spoils with another hacker and spend the next year wondering who really won.

endings.title: Bridge Lockdown
endings.summary: Bulkheads seal, lights go white, and the prison ship reduces your options to one dying hallway.

endings.title: Crown of Ozymandias
endings.summary: You lift the dreadnought's warbook and leave admirals rewriting doctrine around your theft.

endings.title: Particle Wake
endings.summary: The core blossom catches you mid-transit. Your signal survives longer than your body does.

endings.title: A Deal with the Brass
endings.summary: You trade truth for protection and become the navy's favorite criminal asset.

endings.title: Flagship Mutiny
endings.summary: You turn the dreadnought's own command lattice against its officers and sail the chaos out behind you.

endings.title: Fuse Pulled
endings.summary: You cripple the flagship instead of looting it. Historians argue whether that saved more lives than the data ever could.

endings.title: Taken to the Citadel
endings.summary: The flagship captures you intact, which is worse than killing you and far more expensive.

endings.title: Memetic Loop
endings.summary: A thought-weapon hidden in the citadel convinces you to keep opening the same door forever.

endings.title: Burning Ejection
endings.summary: You flee in a command capsule with enough evidence to ruin careers and enough enemies to need a new planet.

endings.title: Ascension of the War-Mind
endings.summary: You wake the dreadnought's strategic mind fully. Humanity becomes one of its smaller optimization problems.

endings.title: Developer Room
endings.summary: You find a maintenance chapel full of impossible comments, prototype art, and one sarcastic blessing.

endings.title: Janus Reset
endings.summary: Janus rewrites fleet identity records from the root. Every ship wakes up doubting its own orders.

endings.title: Chained to Erebus
endings.summary: You merge with the dark archive and become the rumor hackers pray to and fear.

endings.title: Prometheus Unbound
endings.summary: You steal not just secrets but the engine of the regime itself, then give the sector a future it can actually choose.

endings.title: Ghost Bloom
endings.summary: The ghost frequencies flower through military space. Old dead channels start talking back.

endings.title: Sold the Abyss
endings.summary: You auction the Erebus archive in pieces. Civilization gets richer, meaner, and less stable.

endings.title: The Sleeper Fleet
endings.summary: Ancient cold-storage crews wake under your signal. None of them remember the empire that claims them.
